name: Jiva Gosvami

dates_evidence.description: Manuscript colophons across the Ṣaṭ-Sandarbha and Sarva-Saṃvādinī.

core_thesis: Jīva Gosvāmī is the systematic theologian of the Gauḍīya school: he raises Caitanya's experiential teaching and the Gosvāmī corpus's doctrinal motifs into a fully argued *acintya-bhedābheda* metaphysics, complete with epistemology (*pramāṇa*-theory grounded in *śabda* and centred on the *Bhāgavata-Purāṇa*), ontology (*Bhagavān* as *svayaṃ-bhagavān* with three *śaktis* — *svarūpa-śakti* / *cit*, *jīva-śakti* / *taṭasthā*, and *māyā-śakti* / *bahiraṅgā*), and soteriology (*bhakti* as *abhidheya* and *prīti* / *prema* as *prayojana*). The signature concept is *acintya-śakti*: Bhagavān's inconceivable power, by which the *bheda* of *śakti* and *śaktimat* and the *abheda* of their identity in substance hold simultaneously without contradiction, since *acintya-śakti* is precisely what dialectical *tarka* cannot exhaustively adjudicate. The *jīva* is eternal, atomic, and *taṭastha* — constitutively oriented for relation to Bhagavān — neither identical with nor fully separable from him. *Prema*, especially in *mādhurya-rasa*, is the actualisation of this relation in its most intense form.

engaged_works.title: Sat-Sandarbha
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored six-fold systematic Gauḍīya exposition.
engaged_works.summary: The *Ṣaṭ-Sandarbha* is the foundational systematic statement of Caitanya-Vaiṣṇava theology, organised as six interlocking *sandarbhas* ("essays"): *Tattva-Sandarbha* establishes the *pramāṇa* foundation, arguing for *śabda*-pramāṇa with the *Bhāgavata-Purāṇa* as the natural commentary on the *Brahma-Sūtras* and the highest scriptural authority; *Bhagavat-Sandarbha* establishes Bhagavān, with the threefold *śakti*-doctrine — *svarūpa*, *taṭasthā*, *māyā* — and *acintya-śakti* as load-bearing concept; *Paramātma-Sandarbha* treats Bhagavān's immanent aspect as *antaryāmin*, the *jīva*'s ontological status, and the relation of *Brahman* to the cosmos; *Kṛṣṇa-Sandarbha* defends the school-defining *kṛṣṇas tu bhagavān svayam* — Kṛṣṇa as *svayaṃ-bhagavān*, not as *avatāra*; *Bhakti-Sandarbha* analyses *bhakti* as the unique *abhidheya*; *Prīti-Sandarbha* treats *prema* as *prayojana* and the highest *puruṣārtha*. The work is the canonical Gauḍīya systematic theology, the school's reference for every doctrinal question thereafter, and the principal channel by which Caitanya's teaching enters the Sanskrit philosophical mainstream.

engaged_works.title: Sarva-Samvadini
engaged_works.summary: The *Sarva-Saṃvādinī* is Jīva's auto-commentary and supplementary technical treatise on the first four *sandarbhas* of the *Ṣaṭ-Sandarbha*. Where the *sandarbhas* themselves are organised by topic and proceed by a combined doxographical and exegetical method, the *Sarva-Saṃvādinī* sharpens the points where Gauḍīya doctrine is exposed to technical objection from rival Vedānta schools — Advaita, Viśiṣṭādvaita, Madhva-Dvaita, the Nimbārka and Bhāskara *bhedābheda* lines — and supplies the formal *uttarapakṣa*. It engages closely with *pramāṇa*-theoretic and ontological controversies: the status of *śabda*, the nature of *Brahman*'s *śaktis*, the simultaneous *bheda*–*abheda* relation, the *jīva*'s *taṭastha* nature, and the metaphysics of *acintya-śakti*. Read alongside the *Sandarbhas* it forms the Gauḍīya school's mature philosophical instrument, demonstrating that *acintya-bhedābheda* is not a retreat from dialectic but a position that survives sustained Vedāntic cross-examination.
